review_text: I very much enjoyed the 30+ hours I've put into this, I really....REALLY have, but this is not a game. This is a DLC pack for Dying Light 2 that they shoved a bunch of filler into. It's fun, and any fan of the games is going to enjoy it, but I can't recommend it at it's current price point.

Game just doesn't feel fun, Zombies are to tanky, weapons are made of toilet paper, animation locks from zombies happen from like 5 feet away, It might get better but first impression is a pretty hard miss. So finished the game on Burtal, and it just wasn't very fun, the mechanics were shallow you never felt strong, the game just feels like a DLC, honestly if the game was a DLC it would be decent but you can't pass this off as a full game.

Love Dying Light 1 and I wasn't a huge fan of Dying Light 2. This game originally was meant to be a DLC and within the first two hours of gameplay I could notice that. It's not a bad game by any means ripping people apart is awesome but the price definitely isn't worth it, I could notice from the skill tree and the progression very early on that this game would be extremely short. It is definitely an upgrade from the original and dying light 2, but its essentially just the same as Spider Man: Miles Morales where you're getting a pretty short experience for a game that is worth 60 dollars. I would definitely buy this if it was 40 dollars or less though.

The Grab mechanic the zombies have ruins this game. they grab way too often, and it even cancels you if you are mid swing with your melee attack. once it even happend when i used the charge kick on a zombie and it just grabbed me any way, like i didnt even do it in the first place... suuuper annoying.

They make you travel five minutes to talk to someone for two seconds, then travel five minutes back to where you just came from for the previous quest, kill a few zombies, and rinse and repeat that silly loop for 80% of the game.

not sure how this game got such glowing reviews. its more dying light, but it's lost the charm the first game had. its very repetitive, combat start to get monotonous, and zombies non stop grapple. i'd give it a 5.5/10

For 60 dollars, you're getting: a DLC rushed into a full game with very little effort. Its literally just dying light 2 with less features, less exploration, and less variety. Yay Crane is back, filled with plot holes! Yay cars are back, but they're slow, made of cardboard, and completely useless with all the trees in the way! Yay guns are back and with all the ammo you get, even on brutal, why use anything else! Unless its on a sale i wouldn't recommend it. The fact that there are area levels alone should tell you how i feel about this open world zombie survival parkour exploration game. Literally just play dying light 1 or 2.

Reused a ton of assets then charging $60 for it, then cut and resold content which was not included for buying the ultimate edition prior (I can overlook this but I'm not ignoring it). Combat is somehow less responsive than DL2, and parkour feels float-y and slow. Skill tree is extremely simplified as they give you previous skills at the start, but don't fill the gaps so it's barren. Can't sit here and bark that it'll get ironed out, because it shouldn't be happening again, especially for a dlc priced at a triple A game price.

Game runs smooth, similar combat feeling to dl1/2, parkour feels less floaty as well. the beast is fun, but would be better if volatile. OST at first reminds me of kinda DL1 vibes. But still cons -Art Direction, colors and all other stuff look worse than a Nurgle -Bullet sponge everywhere -No scaling whatsoever -Difficulty is there just to be there -Dead Island 2 has a better gore system, and way better feeling weapons -Still no impact on melee weapons. -Asset flipping DL2, animations, boring gameplay, UI design, QTE's -Selling a DL2 DLC worth less than 20€ for 60. -No parkour points -All kinds of bugs for example in parkour it's quite obvious it's not polished

It's a really well done, high quality game, the storytelling is the best out of all Dying Light entries, but why I don't recommend it is that there's just not enough content to justify the price tag. In just over 30 hours of playing I have done everything that there is to do. (and I wasn't even trying to rush it) Unlike other entries in the series, The Beast doesn't give a reason to continue playing after the main story is completed. There is NOTHING to do in the game. Everything is unlocked, everything is finished, If you want to get the best value for your money - get this game when it's discounted.

i love the dying light series, but this one feels kind of a letdown. there's definitely an improvement from dying light 2, parkour and combat, though the same can't be said about the story. the game is so SHORT and for what?? it feels unfinished. maybe i had my expectations too high, but dying light 1 is still miles ahead. that said, it’s not a bad game, but I’d recommend getting it on sale.

Dying Light: The Buggy Beast Just a short list of all the countless bugs me and my friends have encountered during 20 hours of play - Missions that cannot be completed - Missions that cannot be started - Game crashes - Fps drops after doing certain actions, not related to user's hardware - Night/Day cycle completely bugged EVERY time - Falling through the map - Indicators above other player's heads disappearing - Teleportation glitches - Rain indoor and many many more.. How Techland looked at this and went "yeah let's release it one day earlier" is a marvel to me. The core game concept is okay at best but the bugs just push it to being ridiculous, would not recommend as is

I don't understand why this game is on very positive reviews,it's written worse than dying light 2,the gameplay is messier,the optimization sucks i constantly dropped below 20 fps on low with a 3070 and a ryzen 9 5900x this game needs way more time to cook,also constantly needing to be connected to epic and the constant pop ups when you "disconnect" is annoying

Game is boring and story isn't well written, 80% of the game is go from point A to B, talk to this guy and do that boring mission etc. and I feel like they disabled fast travel so the game can feel longer. It's a crime this game doesn't have fast travel. Combat is bad and sloppy, and movement could use a lot of work. Game is really challenging and difficult, BTW. Ending is good, wish the whole game was like the last ~2-3h in the game.

The world is literally dead. Not just because zombies - but because there is no life in it. According to the plot and cutscenes, survivors, crowds of soldiers, scientists, and so on are hiding somewhere around. However, you don't encounter them except during missions where the military is supposed to appear. Nothing happens by itself. Even the rare outposts with soldiers can stand for hours 5 meters from zombies, and everyone will be fine. For a AAA game from 2025, such a cardboard-like world is very noticeable.

Inferior in every way to the old dying light. Even it's graphics are worse, you can count the pixels in blood splatters, the gore is just ripped off from the second game too. Skills are bland and boring. Also they turned Kyle Crane into a ''run when my eyes turn red'' anime character.

Skill tree was mid. Stealth was mid. Music was good. Gun play was OK. Parkour + combat felt jank. Throwables were awesome to use, especially the throwing knife. Grapple hook was terrible. Vehicles were cool. Nighttime chases can still make you wish you wore brown pants. Side quests didn't feel repetitive thankfully, but there definitely wasn't enough of them. Main quests were really cool, especially the later half. Kyle's dialogue still feels like he's a 10 year old child unfortunately, but I'll admit he had a couple really bad ass moments, and a couple super funny ones too. I'd honestly recommend this game if there was just more content.

Absolutely horrific, Within 10 minutes of having unlocked CO-op, me and my friend got stuck on a bridge. 5 minutes later during night time, we were somehow able to hit enemies through the wall getting immense amounts of XP without even visiting town hall. At the boss fight in town hall, we were able to sit on the statue and beat the boss without him even responding. This game will need 'ALOT' of fixes.

It's not fun, that's the main part. The map design seems to forget that this series is a silly parkour them up as most of it is just open terrain with little verticality. When night rolls around you can either hide or get killed by the numerous infected that seem to spawn on you just as you get out of sight. The combat is meaty and has a nice feel to it, for a time, and then constant zombie grapples and bosses with needlessly large health bars sapped any love I had for fighting. On top of all of this, the story is bland and un-inspired. very much not the focus but a further disappointment on top of everything else. Wait for a good sale or just play the first one.

Dying Light 2.5 is a shameless Nostalgia bait. They didn't even try to create a story, the Antagonist is a Far Cry-esque cartoon cutout, parcour is clunky, combat feels outdated, "bosses" are damage-sponges with 3 attack patterns each, crafting relies on you collecting thousands of garbage items, dark zones are worthless copy paste dungeons, cars are completely out of place and exist purely because they existed before, etc. But hey, Kyle Crane is back, with a voice actor who's half asleep while reading his lines. Great It's a worse version of Dead Island 2.
